---
title: "CombinedProbs"
author: "Daniel Myung"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Create a named list of your variables
check_lengths <- list(
  logreg = p_logreg,
  knn    = p_knn,
  ann    = p_ann,
  svm    = p_svm,
  tree   = p_tree,
  actual = y_true
)

# Check the length of each
sapply(check_lengths, length)
```

```{r}
# Load the dummy test set to get the actual outcomes
test_dummy <- readRDS("test_dummy.rds")

# Extract the actual target (using the same index as your previous steps)
# Based on your previous code, the target was at ncol - 3
y_true <- test_dummy[, ncol(test_dummy) - 3]

# Load the probability vectors generated by your models
p_logreg <- readRDS("glm_prob.rds")
p_knn    <- readRDS("knn_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_ann    <- readRDS("ann_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_svm    <- readRDS("rbf_prob_mental_illness.rds")
p_tree   <- readRDS("c50_prob_mental_illness.rds")

# Combine the vectors and the actual response
stack_df <- data.frame(
  logreg = p_logreg,
  knn    = p_knn,
  ann    = p_ann,
  svm    = p_svm,
  tree   = p_tree,
  actual = as.factor(y_true)
)

# Check the numeric spread of your experts
summary(stack_df)

# Check the first 20 rows to ensure alignment
head(stack_df, 20)
```

```{r}
length(p_logreg)
length(p_knn)
length(p_ann)
length(p_svm)
length(p_tree)
length(y_true)
```
